items.body:
### Bug Description

n8n Version2.6.3

Since update about a week ago ( could have been only few versions, I update 2-3x a month) I am noticing that each publish will register the webhook in gitlab agian, with same URL and make the workflow be activated multiple times in the same second.



### To Reproduce

Use n8n Version2.6.3 and publish workflow with GL webhook multiple times

### Expected behavior

Only one webhook should be active per workflow / UUID

### Debug Info

# Debug info

items.title: n8n export:entities fails when secrets_provider_connection is not configured

labels.description: Good first issue for starting with contributions

labels.name: status:in-linear

items.body:
### Bug Description

I am running n8n `sha256:a9beb0dcaa547f0742a322f497af72127338d6ab8f3697b3be44f8ab737726f2` (non-enterprise) and am seeing this error when creating a backup

```
📊 Processing table: secrets_provider_connection (secretsproviderconnection)
   💭 Columns: updatedAt, createdAt, providerKey, type, encryptedSettings, isEnabled
❌ Error exporting entities. See log messages for details. 

Error details:

====================================

relation "secrets_provider_connection" does not exist 

relation "secrets_provider_connection" does not exist
```

### To Reproduce

1. pull down the above image
2. without defining `secrets_provider_connection`, run `n8n export:entities --outputDir=/backup/entities --includeExecutionHistoryDataTables=true`

### Expected behavior

That step should produce a warning or be skipped altogether if the connection hasn't been defined. 

### Debug Info

# Debug info

items.title: dateTime.format() value will display on output field but when executed returns invalid DateTime

labels.name: triage:pending
labels.description: Waiting to be triaged

labels.name: Good First Issue
labels.description: Good first issue for starting with contributions

labels.name: status:in-linear
labels.description: Issue or PR is now in Linear

items.body: ### Bug Description Hello, the previous [ticket](https://github.com/n8n-io/n8n/issues/23215) was closed due to inactivity, but the issue is still there. DateTime.fromISO() works when editing a node, but doesn't work when full workflow is executed ### To Reproduce 1. Paste my simple test workflow `{ "nodes": [ { "parameters": {}, "type": "n8n-nodes-base.manualTrigger", "typeVersion": 1, "position": [ 0, 0 ], "id": "89517ad5-18cc-43a2-8d60-23a6663fb776", "name": "When clicking ‘Execute workflow’" }, { "parameters": { "operation": "get", "dataTableId": { "__rl": true, "value": "RTUmXcXt5SSkmZr6", "mode": "list", "cachedResultName": "test2", "cachedResultUrl": "/projects/Gf5SWUTiJ2Xzg8or/datatables/RTUmXcXt5SSkmZr6" }, "limit": 1 }, "type": "n8n-nodes-base.dataTable", "typeVersion": 1, "position": [ 208, 0 ], "id": "4606fdc5-27a7-4bee-899b-35d3576ec415", "name": "Get row(s)" }, { "parameters": { "assignments": { "assignments": [ { "id": "3f58c229-f02b-4c92-8b10-0c7ecbc2b7bb", "name": "timestamp", "value": "={{ DateTime.fromISO($json.updatedAt).toFormat('dd/MM/yyyy HH:mm:ss') }}", "type": "string" }, { "id": "8b656ed7-bed8-4b48-89a8-b8e4480f774f", "name": "tt", "value": "={{ $json.updatedAt.toDateTime().setZone('Europe/Moscow').toFormat('dd/MM/yyyy HH:mm:ss') }}", "type": "string" } ] }, "includeOtherFields": true, "options": {} }, "type": "n8n-nodes-base.set", "typeVersion": 3.4, "position": [ 416, 0 ], "id": "69cf11a6-ef24-40e8-b73b-fe27b1adc5a4", "name": "Add timestamp" } ], "connections": { "When clicking ‘Execute workflow’": { "main": [ [ { "node": "Get row(s)", "type": "main", "index": 0 } ] ] }, "Get row(s)": { "main": [ [ { "node": "Add timestamp", "type": "main", "index": 0 } ] ] }, "Add timestamp": { "main": [ [] ] } }, "pinData": {}, "meta": { "instanceId": "435a1ecb9e606b9753a6ec0496715a8d59ac35a388e07f71b8ed8bb128b2d349" } }` 2. In the **get rows** node choose any table you have 3. Run the workflow 4. Check the output of the **add timestamp** node - you'll see "timestamp" field has "Invalid DateTime" output 5. Open editor of the **add timestamp** node 6. Click "Execute step" button - now you have "timestamp" field with a proper datetime ### Expected behavior I expect DateTime.fromISO() to work properly both in editor and when I execute the full workflow ### Debug Info # Debug info ## core - n8nVersion: 2.6.3 - platform: docker (self-hosted) - nodeJsVersion: 22.22.0 - nodeEnv: production - database: sqlite - executionMode: regular - concurrency: -1 - license: enterprise (production) - consumerId: bf26dc67-2fd6-475f-9ae6-cdd0af7ee804 ## storage - success: all - error: all - progress: false - manual: true - binaryMode: filesystem ## pruning - enabled: true - maxAge: 336 hours - maxCount: 10000 executions ## client - userAgent: mozilla/5.0 (windows nt 10.0; win64; x64) applewebkit/537.36 (khtml, like gecko) chrome/144.0.0.0 safari/537.36 - isTouchDevice: false ## security - secureCookie: false Generated at: 2026-02-03T21:01:25.477Z ### Operating System Debian 12 ### n8n Version 2.6.3 ### Node.js Version 22.22.0 ### Database SQLite (default) ### Execution mode main (default) ### Hosting self hosted

items.title: YouTube Upload node ignores binary mimeType and sends application/mp4 instead of video/mp4

labels.name: triage:pending
labels.description: Waiting to be triaged

labels.name: Good First Issue
labels.description: Good first issue for starting with contributions

labels.name: status:in-linear
labels.description: Issue or PR is now in Linear

items.body: ### Bug Description After updating from n8n 1.1.18.2 to 2.3.5: YouTube Upload node sends incorrect MIME type application/mp4 even when incoming binary data has MIME type video/mp4. This causes YouTube API to reject uploads with: "Media type 'application/mp4' is not supported." Read file from disk node: ### To Reproduce 1. Run n8n in Docker (latest version) 2. Use Read/Write Files from Disk node to read an MP4 file 3. Set Mime Type option to video/mp4 4. Verify Binary tab shows video/mp4 5. Pass binary to YouTube Upload node 6. Execute workflow ### Expected behavior YouTube Upload node should respect incoming binary mimeType or correctly set video/mp4 when uploading MP4 files. ### Debug Info # Debug info ## core - n8nVersion: 2.3.5 - platform: docker (self-hosted) - nodeJsVersion: 22.21.1 - nodeEnv: production - database: postgres - executionMode: scaling (single-main) - concurrency: -1 - license: enterprise (production) - consumerId: 2e4f3a1c-0800-4dbb-a301-5a5320a22cb6 ## storage - success: all - error: all - progress: false - manual: true - binaryMode: filesystem ## pruning - enabled: true - maxAge: 168 hours - maxCount: 10000 executions ## client - userAgent: mozilla/5.0 (windows nt 10.0; win64; x64; rv:146.0) gecko/20100101 firefox/146.0 - isTouchDevice: false Generated at: 2026-01-14T20:31:03.677Z ### Operating System Ubuntu 22.04 ### n8n Version 2.3.5 ### Node.js Version 22.21.1 ### Database PostgreSQL ### Execution mode queue ### Hosting self hosted

items.title: Send email node always mark images as inline attachments

labels.name: triage:pending
labels.description: Waiting to be triaged

labels.name: Good First Issue
labels.description: Good first issue for starting with contributions

labels.name: status:in-linear
labels.description: Issue or PR is now in Linear

items.body:
### Bug Description

Send email node always marks images as inline attachments, regardless of whether they are referenced in the body of the message or not, and it is not possible to alter this behavior. Microsoft Exchange server leaves them in the message body, but MS Outlook or OWA clients do not display them.

### To Reproduce

Create binary data (f.e. jpeg image) with appropriate MimeType image/jpeg and name xxx.jpg.
Add them to attachment in Send email node.

### Expected behavior

Either explicitly allow the option to choose which ones should or should not be inline, or better yet, automatically scan the body of the message and mark only those that are referenced.

### Debug Info

# Debug info

items.body: ## Describe the problem/error/question I want to create Miro OAuth2 API credentials. I fill the Client ID and Client Secret fields, click on the "connect my account" button and get 400 error "The field "scope" is mandatory for credentials of type "miroOAuth2Api" ## What is the error message (if any)? "The field "scope" is mandatory for credentials of type "miroOAuth2Api" ## Please share your workflow/screenshots/recording ``` (Select the nodes on your canvas and use the keyboard shortcuts CMD+C/CTRL+C and CMD+V/CTRL+V to copy and paste the workflow.) ⚠️ WARNING ⚠️ If you have sensitive data in your workflow (like API keys), please remove it before sharing. ``` ## Share the output returned by the last node ## Debug info ### core - n8nVersion: 1.123.5 - platform: docker (self-hosted) - nodeJsVersion: 22.21.0 - nodeEnv: production - database: postgres - executionMode: scaling (multi-main) - concurrency: -1 - license: enterprise (production) ### storage - success: all - error: all - progress: false - manual: true - binaryMode: s3 ### pruning - enabled: true - maxAge: 750 hours - maxCount: 0 executions ### client - userAgent: mozilla/5.0 (windows nt 10.0; win64; x64) applewebkit/537.36 (khtml, like gecko) chrome/143.0.0.0 safari/537.36 - isTouchDevice: false Generated at: 2026-01-14T10:02:51.534Z}
